name: Sunbeam Swiss Steak
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Round steak; 1 to 1-1/2 inches thick (to 3 pounds)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the steak on a breadboard and pound both sides with flour until absorbed.
2. Preheat the frypan to 280 degrees F. Add fat.
3. When the fat is melted, add onions and sauté until golden (about 3 minutes). Remove from fat.
4. Set dial to 360 degrees F. Add 2 tablespoons additional fat.
5. When hot, add meat and brown well on both sides (10 to 15 minutes), increasing heat if necessary.
6. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
7. Spread sautéed onions, garlic, celery, tomatoes, and pepper slices over the steak.
8. Cover and turn dial to simmering point. Simmer until tender, about 1-1/2 hours.
9. Remove meat to a hot platter and skim off fat from drippings.
10. Add a few drops of bottled gravy sauce (to taste) and pour over the meat.
11. Sprinkle with chopped parsley. Serve with hot noodles, rice, or potatoes.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Tomatoes may be omitted, and beef bouillon may be added instead. Sauteed or canned mushrooms can be added a short time before cooking is complete. Peeled potatoes and carrots may be added about 40 minutes before cooking is complete if large, cut in half.
